title: From EduVisBench to EduVisAgent: A Benchmark and Multi-Agent Framework for Reasoning-Driven Pedagogical Visualization
abstract: While foundation models (FMs), such as diffusion models and large vision-language models (LVLMs), have been widely applied in educational contexts, their ability to generate pedagogically effective visual explanations remains limited. Most existing approaches focus primarily on textual reasoning, overlooking the critical role of structured and interpretable visualizations in supporting conceptual understanding. To better assess the visual reasoning capabilities of FMs in educational settings, we introduce EduVisBench, a multi-domain, multi-level benchmark. EduVisBench features diverse STEM problem sets requiring visually grounded solutions, along with a fine-grained evaluation rubric informed by pedagogical theory. Our empirical analysis reveals that existing models frequently struggle with the inherent challenge of decomposing complex reasoning and translating it into visual representations aligned with human cognitive processes. To address these limitations, we propose EduVisAgent, a multi-agent collaborative framework that coordinates specialized agents for instructional planning, reasoning decomposition, metacognitive prompting, and visualization design. Experimental results show that EduVisAgent substantially outperforms all baselines, achieving a 40.2% improvement and delivering more educationally aligned visualizations.
explain_zh:
- 问题：现有大模型在生成教学可视化方面能力不足。
- 动机：教学可视化对理解概念至关重要，但现有模型多关注文本，忽视视觉表达。
- 方法：提出EduVisBench评测基准和EduVisAgent多智能体框架，分步协作生成可视化。
- 结论：EduVisAgent显著优于基线，提升40.2%，生成更符合教学逻辑的可视化。
- 创新点：首个面向教学可视化的多智能体框架，结合教育理论与多智能体协作。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 现有大模型难以生成真正有助于学生理解的、符合教学逻辑的可视化内容。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 教学可视化能显著提升概念理解，但当前模型多聚焦文本推理，忽视视觉表达的教育价值。  
- 缺乏评估模型生成可视化质量的标准化工具，导致研究难以推进。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 构建EduVisBench：涵盖多学科、多难度的STEM问题集，配有基于教育理论的细粒度评分标准。  
- 设计EduVisAgent：由多个专业智能体协作，分别负责教学规划、分步推理、自我反思和可视化设计。  
- 采用“分而治之”策略，将复杂问题拆解，逐层生成符合认知规律的可视化。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- EduVisAgent在EduVisBench上显著优于所有基线模型，性能提升达40.2%。  
- 生成的可视化更清晰、结构合理，更贴近教师教学逻辑，适合真实教育场景使用。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首个专为教学可视化设计的多智能体框架，实现“教育理论+智能体协作”深度融合。  
- 提出首个系统性评测基准EduVisBench，推动该领域评估标准化。

title: Aurelius: Relation Aware Text-to-Audio Generation At Scale
abstract: We present Aurelius, a new framework that enables relation aware text-to-audio (TTA) generation research at scale. Given the lack of essential audio event and relation corpora, \emph{Aurelius} contributes a large-scale audio event corpus \emph{AudioEventSet} and another large-scale relation corpus \emph{AudioRelSet}. Comprising 110 event categories, AudioEventSet maximally covers all commonly heard audio events and each event is unique, realistic and of high-quality. AudioRelSet consists of 100 relations, comprehensively covering the relations that present in the physical world or can be neatly described by text.  As the two corpora provide audio event and relation independently, they can be combined to create massive <text,audio> pairs with our pair generation strategy to support relation aware TTA investigation at scale. We comprehensively benchmark all existing TTA models from both general and relation aware evaluation perspective. We further provide in-depth investigation on scaling up existing TTA models' relation aware generation by either training from scratch or leveraging cross-domain general TTA knowledge. The introduced corpora and the findings through investigation in this work potentially facilitate future research on relation aware TTA generation.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 实现大规模、关系感知的文本到音频生成（TTA）研究。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有文本到音频生成研究缺乏高质量、大规模的音频事件与关系数据集，限制了对“关系感知”生成能力的探索。
  - 无法有效评估模型是否真正理解文本中描述的事件间逻辑关系（如“敲门”与“开门”）。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建两个大规模独立数据集：AudioEventSet（110类真实音频事件）和 AudioRelSet（100种物理/文本可描述的关系）。
  - 采用组合策略，将事件与关系自由搭配，生成海量 <文本, 音频> 对，支持关系感知的TTA训练与评估。
  - 在此基础上，对现有TTA模型进行系统性评估与扩展，探索从头训练或迁移学习提升关系感知能力。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 首次实现大规模关系感知TTA研究，显著提升模型对复杂语义关系的生成能力。
  - 实验表明，利用跨域通用TTA知识可有效提升关系感知生成效果。
  - 适用于未来关系感知音频生成、多模态理解与生成等研究方向。

  - 首次提出并构建独立的音频事件与关系数据集，实现灵活组合与大规模生成。
  - 提出“关系感知TTA”新研究范式，推动从“字面生成”向“语义理解生成”演进。

title: AutoCodeBench: Large Language Models are Automatic Code Benchmark Generators
abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown impressive performance across diverse domains, with code generation emerging as a particularly prominent application. However, existing benchmarks designed to evaluate code generation exhibit several critical limitations. First, most rely on manual annotations, which are time-consuming and difficult to scale across programming languages and problem complexities. Second, the majority focus primarily on Python, while the few multilingual benchmarks suffer from limited difficulty and imbalanced language coverage. 
To overcome these challenges, we present AutoCodeGen, an automated framework for constructing high-difficulty, multilingual code generation datasets without manual annotations. Our approach guarantees correctness and completeness by generating test inputs with LLMs, obtaining test outputs within a multilingual sandbox, and further enhancing quality through reverse problem generation and multi-stage filtering. 
Based on this novel method, we introduce AutoCodeBench, a large-scale benchmark suite spanning 20 programming languages with balanced coverage. AutoCodeBench is designed to rigorously evaluate LLMs on diverse, challenging, and realistic multilingual programming tasks. Extensive experiments reveal that even state-of-the-art models struggle on these tasks, particularly in low-resource languages.
Besides, we release complementary training and evaluation resources, including a large-scale, verifiable multilingual instruction dataset generated via the same pipeline, as well as a multilingual sandbox with high-concurrency support. We hope these contributions will provide a solid foundation for future research and inspire the community to explore more automatic and scalable approaches to multilingual code generation, with a particular emphasis on advancing progress in low-resource languages.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何自动构建高质量、多语言、高难度的代码生成评估基准，摆脱对人工标注的依赖。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有代码生成基准大多依赖人工标注，成本高、难扩展；且主要集中在Python，缺乏多语言平衡与挑战性。
  - 低资源编程语言的评估严重不足，限制了模型在真实多语言场景下的能力评估。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 利用大模型自动生成测试输入，并在多语言沙箱中运行获取正确输出。
  - 通过“反向问题生成”（从输出反推问题）和多阶段过滤提升数据质量与难度。
  - 构建自动化流水线，实现从问题生成到验证的端到端闭环。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 成功构建覆盖20种语言的AutoCodeBench，语言分布均衡，任务难度高。
  - 即使顶尖模型在该基准上表现不佳，尤其在低资源语言中问题突出。
  - 适用于评估和训练多语言代码生成模型，尤其推动低资源语言发展。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次实现无需人工标注的全自动多语言代码基准生成框架（AutoCodeGen）。
  - 提出“反向问题生成”与多阶段过滤机制，显著提升数据质量与挑战性。
  - 开源配套资源（多语言沙箱、指令数据集），推动社区可持续发展。

title: Battery Fault: A Comprehensive Dataset and Benchmark for Battery Fault Diagnosis
abstract: With the accelerated popularization of electric vehicles (EV), battery safety issues have become an important research focus. Data-driven battery fault diagnosis algorithms, built on real-world operational data, are critical methods for reducing safety risks. However, existing battery datasets have limitations such as insufficient scale, coarse-grained labels, and lack of coverage of real-world operating conditions, which seriously restrict the development of data-driven fault diagnosis algorithms. To address these issues, this paper introduces a large-scale benchmark dataset named CH-BatteryGen, which is, to the best of our knowledge, the first EV battery system fault diagnosis dataset based on real-world operating conditions. This dataset integrates real on-board operation data with mechanism-constrained generative modeling technology, balancing authenticity and scalability. It covers two mainstream battery chemistries, namely nickel-cobalt-manganese (NCM) lithium batteries and lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries, and involves charging, discharging, and operation data of 1000 electric vehicles. It provides four fault labels (normal, self-discharge, high-resistance, low-capacity) and three severity level annotations, supporting two benchmark tasks: fault classification and fault grading. Through systematic validation using traditional machine learning methods (random forest (RF), support vector machine (SVM)) and deep learning models (long short-term memory (LSTM), convolutional neural network (CNN)), the results show that the CNN model performs best in the fault classification task, achieving an F1-score of 0.9280 in the LFP discharging scenario; in the fault grading task, the F1-score reaches 0.8813. The CH-BatteryGen dataset has been open-sourced, aiming to provide a standardized evaluation platform for battery fault diagnosis algorithms, promote research development in this field, and contribute to the transformation of sustainable transportation systems.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 构建一个大规模、真实场景下的电池故障诊断数据集，以支持更可靠的电池安全研究。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有电池数据集规模小、标签粗糙、缺乏真实运行环境覆盖，限制了数据驱动故障诊断算法的发展。
  - 电动汽车普及加速，电池安全成为关键问题，亟需高质量数据支撑算法研究。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 采用真实车载运行数据与机制约束的生成建模技术相结合，提升数据真实性与可扩展性。
  - 覆盖1000辆电动车，包含NCM和LFP两种主流电池化学体系，涵盖充放电及运行全过程。
  - 提供四种故障类型（正常、自放电、高阻、低容量）及三级严重程度标签，支持分类与分级双任务。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - CNN模型在LFP放电场景下故障分类F1-score达0.9280，故障分级F1-score达0.8813，表现最优。
  - 数据集已开源，可作为电池故障诊断算法的标准化评测平台。
  - 适用于电池安全研究、智能诊断系统开发及电动汽车可持续发展支持。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首个基于真实运行条件的EV电池故障诊断数据集，兼具真实性和规模性。
  - 融合真实数据与生成建模，突破传统数据集在真实性和可扩展性间的矛盾。
  - 提供细粒度故障标签与多任务支持，推动故障诊断从“有无故障”迈向“严重程度评估”。

title: CitySeeker: How Do VLMs Explore Embodied Urban Navigation with Implicit Human Needs?
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have made significant progress in explicit instruction-based navigation; however, their ability to interpret implicit human needs (e.g., ''I am thirsty'') in dynamic urban environments remains underexplored. This paper introduces CitySeeker, a novel benchmark designed to assess VLMs’ spatial reasoning and decision-making capabilities for exploring embodied urban navigation to address implicit needs. CitySeeker includes 6,440 trajectories across 8 cities, capturing diverse visual characteristics and implicit needs in 7 goal-driven scenarios. Extensive experiments reveal that even top-performing models (e.g., Qwen2.5-VL-32B-Instruct) achieve only 21.1% task completion. We find key bottlenecks in error accumulation in long-horizon reasoning, inadequate spatial cognition, and deficient experiential recall. To further analyze them, we investigate a series of exploratory strategies—Backtracking Mechanisms, Enriching Spatial Cognition, and Memory-Based Retrieval (BCR), inspired by human cognitive mapping's emphasis on iterative observation-reasoning cycles and adaptive path optimization. Our analysis provides actionable insights for developing VLMs with robust spatial intelligence required for tackling ''last-mile'' navigation challenges.

- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 探索视觉语言模型（VLMs）在城市环境中理解并响应人类隐性需求（如“我渴了”）进行自主导航的能力。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有VLMs擅长处理明确指令（如“去咖啡馆”），但对隐性需求（如“我渴了”）缺乏理解，难以完成真实城市中“最后一公里”的智能导航任务。
  - 城市环境复杂多变，需模型具备空间推理与经验记忆能力，当前研究尚未系统评估此能力。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建CitySeeker基准：包含6,440条真实城市轨迹，覆盖8个城市、7种目标场景，涵盖多样视觉特征与隐性需求。
  - 提出BCR三策略分析框架：Backtracking（回溯修正）、Enriching Spatial Cognition（增强空间认知）、Memory-Based Retrieval（基于记忆检索），模拟人类“观察-推理-调整”循环。
  - 通过实验分析模型在长程推理、空间理解、记忆召回方面的瓶颈。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）

  - 长程推理误差累积、空间认知薄弱、经验记忆缺失是主要瓶颈。
  - 该基准与分析框架可指导未来模型设计，适用于城市级“最后一公里”智能导航场景。

title: CodeSense: a Real-World Benchmark and Dataset for Code Semantic Reasoning
abstract: Understanding and reasoning about code semantics is essential for enhancing code LLMs' abilities to solve real-world software engineering (SE) tasks. Although several code reasoning benchmarks exist, most rely on synthetic datasets or educational coding problems and focus on coarse-grained reasoning tasks such as input/output prediction, limiting their effectiveness in evaluating LLMs in practical SE contexts. To bridge this gap, we propose CodeSense, the first benchmark that makes available a spectrum of fine-grained code reasoning tasks concerned with the software engineering of real-world code. We collected Python, C and Java software projects from real-world repositories. We executed tests from these repositories, collected their execution traces, and constructed a ground truth dataset for fine-grained semantic reasoning tasks.  We then performed comprehensive evaluations on state-of-the-art LLMs. Our results show a clear performance gap for the models to handle fine-grained reasoning tasks. Although prompting techniques such as chain-of-thought and in-context learning helped, the lack of code semantics in LLMs fundamentally limit models' capabilities of code reasoning. Besides dataset, benchmark and evaluation, our work produced an execution tracing framework and tool set that make it easy to collect ground truth for fine-grained SE reasoning tasks, offering a strong basis for future benchmark construction and model post training. Our code and data are located at \url{https://codesense-bench.github.io/}.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 构建一个面向真实世界软件工程场景的细粒度代码语义理解基准与数据集。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有代码推理基准多基于合成数据或教学题目，任务粗粒度，无法真实反映大模型在实际开发中的表现。
  - 真实软件工程任务需要对代码语义进行细粒度理解，现有评估体系严重不足。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 从真实开源项目（Python/C/Java）中收集代码。
  - 执行项目测试用例，获取运行时执行轨迹（execution traces）。
  - 基于执行轨迹构建细粒度语义推理任务的真值数据集。
  - 设计并评估多种主流大模型在该基准上的表现，对比不同提示策略（如思维链、上下文学习）的效果。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 大模型在细粒度代码语义推理任务上表现显著不足，存在明显能力鸿沟。
  - 提示技术（如思维链）有一定帮助，但无法弥补模型缺乏代码语义理解的本质缺陷。
  - 该基准适用于评估和提升代码大模型在真实开发场景中的推理能力。

  - 首个聚焦真实世界软件工程场景的细粒度代码语义推理基准与数据集。
  - 提出基于执行轨迹构建真值数据的系统化方法，支持未来基准构建。

title: From Conversation to Query Execution: Benchmarking User and Tool Interactions for EHR Database Agents
abstract: Despite the impressive performance of LLM-powered agents, their adoption for Electronic Health Record (EHR) data access remains limited by the absence of benchmarks that adequately capture real-world clinical data access flows. In practice, two core challenges hinder deployment: query ambiguity from vague user questions and value mismatch between user terminology and database entries. To address this, we introduce EHR-ChatQA an interactive database question answering benchmark that evaluates the end-to-end workflow of database agents: clarifying user questions, using tools to resolve value mismatches, and generating correct SQL to deliver accurate answers. To cover diverse patterns of query ambiguity and value mismatch, EHR-ChatQA assesses agents in a simulated environment with an LLM-based user across two interaction flows: Incremental Query Refinement (IncreQA), where users add constraints to existing queries, and Adaptive Query Refinement (AdaptQA), where users adjust their search goals mid-conversation. Experiments with state-of-the-art LLMs (e.g., o4-mini and Gemini-2.5-Flash) over five i.i.d. trials show that while agents achieve high Pass@5 of 90-95% (at least one of five trials) on IncreQA and 60-80% on AdaptQA, their Pass^5 (consistent success across all five trials) is substantially lower by 35-60%. These results underscore the need to build agents that are not only performant but also robust for the safety-critical EHR domain. Finally, we provide diagnostic insights into common failure modes to guide future agent development.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 缺乏能真实反映临床数据访问流程的基准测试，限制了大语言模型（LLM）代理在电子病历（EHR）数据库中的实际应用。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 临床场景中用户提问常模糊不清，且用户术语与数据库术语不匹配，导致现有LLM代理难以准确执行查询。
  - 现有评估方法无法全面衡量代理在真实交互流程中的表现，尤其在多轮对话和动态调整目标时的稳定性。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建EHR-ChatQA基准，模拟真实临床交互：通过LLM扮演用户，与数据库代理进行对话。
  - 设计两种交互模式：增量查询优化（IncreQA）——逐步添加条件；自适应查询优化（AdaptQA）——中途改变搜索目标。
  - 评估代理在澄清问题、调用工具解决术语不一致、生成正确SQL等方面的端到端能力。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 代理在IncreQA上Pass@5达90-95%，但在AdaptQA上仅为60-80%。
  - Pass^5（五次试验均成功）显著更低，降幅达35-60%，说明当前代理在复杂场景下缺乏鲁棒性。
  - 适用于评估和改进面向EHR的LLM代理，尤其在安全关键的医疗场景中。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首个聚焦EHR数据库交互全流程的基准，涵盖真实临床中的模糊提问与术语不匹配问题。
  - 引入两种动态交互模式（IncreQA与AdaptQA），更贴近临床实际对话行为。
  - 提供失败模式诊断，为未来代理设计提供可操作的改进方向。

title: HUME: Measuring the Human-Model Performance Gap in Text Embedding Tasks
abstract:
Comparing human and model performance offers a valuable perspective for understanding the strengths and limitations of embedding models, highlighting wherethey succeed and where they fail to capture meaning and nuance. However, such comparisons are rarely made, as human performance on embedding tasks is difficult to measure. To fill this gap, we introduce HUME: Human Evaluation Framework for Text Embeddings. While frameworks like MTEB provide broad model evaluation, they lack reliable estimates of human performance, limiting the interpretability of model scores. We measure human performance across 16 MTEB datasets spanning reranking, classification, clustering, and semantic textual similarity across linguistically diverse high- and low-resource languages. Humans achieve an average performance of 77.6% compared to 80.1% for the best embedding model, though with substantial variation: models reach high performance on some datasets while struggling on notably low-resource languages. Our human annotation also reveals multiple notable dataset issues. We also benchmark nine LLMs as annotators, finding they fall short of human performance (76.1% vs. 81.2%) despite offering scalability advantages. We provide human performance baselines, insights into task difficulty patterns, and an extensible evaluation framework that enables a more meaningful interpretation of the model and informs the development of both
models and benchmarks.

  - 人类表现是衡量模型是否真正“理解”语义的重要参照，尤其在低资源语言和复杂语义任务中更显关键。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出HUME框架，系统性地收集人类在16个MTEB任务上的标注数据。
  - 任务覆盖重排序、分类、聚类、语义相似度，涵盖高/低资源语言。

title: MATH-Beyond: A Benchmark for RL to Expand Beyond the Base Model
abstract: With the advent of DeepSeek-R1, a new wave of reinforcement learning (RL) methods has emerged that seem to unlock stronger mathematical reasoning. However, a closer look at the open-source ecosystem reveals a critical limitation: with sufficiently many draws (e.g., $\texttt{pass@1024}$), existing base models already solve nearly all questions on widely used math benchmarks such as MATH-500 and AIME 2024. This suggests that the RL fine-tuning methods prevalent in the LLM reasoning literature largely sharpen existing solution modes rather than discovering entirely new ones. Such sharpening stands in contrast to the broader promise of RL: to foster exploration and to acquire new skills. To move beyond this plateau, we introduce MATH-Beyond (MATH-B), a benchmark deliberately constructed to defeat common open-source models of up to 8B parameters even under large sampling budgets. Improving performance on our benchmark via RL requires methods that learn to reason in ways that go beyond base model capabilities in repeated sampling. Since the problems are drawn from subsets of DAPO-Math-17K and DeepScaleR datasets, they remain topically equivalent to standard high-school math. Validating our premise, RL fine-tuned models such as Nemotron-Research-Reasoning-Qwen-1.5B and DeepScaleR-1.5B-Preview perform poorly on MATH-B at $\texttt{pass@1024}$, showing how existing approaches fall short on tackling harder instances. We hope MATH-B will catalyze exploration-driven RL approaches that elicit deeper reasoning capabilities.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何让强化学习（RL）在数学推理任务中突破现有模型的能力瓶颈，发现超越基础模型的新解题方法。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有RL方法在主流数学基准上表现优异，但主要是在“打磨”已有解题思路，而非探索新方法。
  - 当前基准（如MATH-500）在大量采样下，基础模型几乎能全解，导致RL无法真正推动能力跃迁。
  - 真正的RL应鼓励探索与新技能生成，而非仅优化已有路径。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建MATH-Benchmark（MATH-B）：从DAPO-Math-17K和DeepScaleR中筛选出基础模型难以解决的问题。
  - 问题保持与高中数学一致，但难度更高，需突破常规解法。
  - 通过大采样（如pass@1024）测试模型表现，评估是否具备“超越基础模型”的能力。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 即使在1024次采样下，主流RL模型（如Nemotron、DeepScaleR）在MATH-B上表现仍很差。
  - 证明现有RL方法无法有效应对真正困难的问题，存在能力天花板。
  - 适用于推动探索型RL研究，促进模型发展出全新推理模式。

  - 提出“超越基础模型”的新评估范式：不以解题率为目标，而以“能否发现新解法”为标准。
  - 构建首个专为挑战基础模型极限而设计的数学推理基准（MATH-B）。

title: SkyEvents: A Large-Scale Event-enhanced UAV Dataset for Robust 3D Scene Reconstruction
abstract: Recent advances in large-scale 3D scene reconstruction using unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) have spurred increasing interest in neural rendering techniques. However, existing approaches with conventional cameras struggle to capture consistent multi-view images of scenes, particularly in extremely blurred and low-light environments, due to the inherent limitations in dynamic range caused by long exposure and motion blur resulting from camera motion. As a promising solution, bio-inspired event cameras exhibit robustness in extreme scenarios, thanks to their high dynamic range and microsecond-level temporal resolution. Nevertheless, dedicated event datasets specifically tailored for large-scale UAV 3D scene reconstruction remain limited. To bridge this gap, we introduce \textbf{SkyEvents}, a pioneering large-scale event-enhanced UAV dataset for 3D scene reconstruction, incorporating RGB, event, and LiDAR data. SkyEvents encompasses 22 sequences, spanning over 8 hours of video, captured across a diverse set of illumination conditions, scenarios, and flight altitudes. To facilitate the event-based 3D scene reconstruction with SkyEvents, we propose the Geometry-constrained Timestamp Alignment (\textbf{GTA}) module to align timestamps between the event and RGB cameras. Furthermore, we introduce Region-wise Event Rendering (\textbf{RER}) loss for supervising the rendering optimization. With SkyEvents, we aim to motivate and equip researchers to advance large-scale 3D scene reconstruction in challenging environments, harnessing the unique strengths of event cameras. Dataset and code will be available at \url{https://}.

- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在极端光照和运动模糊环境下，实现鲁棒的大规模无人机3D场景重建。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统相机在低光、高速运动下易出现模糊和曝光不足，难以获取一致的多视角图像。
  - 事件相机具有高动态范围和微秒级响应速度，适合极端环境，但缺乏专门用于无人机3D重建的大规模数据集。
  - 现有方法缺乏对事件数据与RGB图像的精准时间对齐和有效监督机制。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建了SkyEvents数据集：包含22个飞行序列、8小时以上数据，融合RGB、事件和LiDAR信息，覆盖多种光照、场景和飞行高度。
  - 提出GTA模块：通过几何约束实现事件与RGB图像的时间戳对齐，提升多源数据同步精度。
  - 设计RER损失函数：按区域划分事件信号，指导神经渲染过程中的图像生成，增强重建质量。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）

  - GTA与RER显著提升重建精度，尤其在低光和动态场景中表现更优。
  - 适用于复杂、极端环境下的无人机3D建模，推动智能感知与导航发展。

title: SpatiaLab: Can Vision–Language Models Perform Spatial Reasoning in the Wild?
abstract:
Spatial reasoning is a fundamental aspect of human cognition, yet it remains a major challenge for contemporary vision–language models (VLMs). Prior work largely relied on synthetic or LLM-generated environments with limited task designs and puzzle-like setups, failing to capture the real-world complexity, visual noise, and diverse spatial relationships that VLMs encounter. To address this, we introduce **_SpatiaLab_**, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating VLMs’ spatial reasoning in realistic, unconstrained contexts.
**_SpatiaLab_** comprises 1,400 visual question–answer pairs across six major categories: *Relative Positioning, Depth & Occlusion, Orientation, Size & Scale, Spatial Navigation,* and *3D Geometry*, each with five subcategories, yielding 30 distinct task types. Each subcategory contains at least 25 questions, and each main category includes at least 200 questions, supporting both multiple-choice and open-ended evaluation.
Experiments across diverse state-of-the-art VLMs, including open- and closed-source models, reasoning-focused, and specialized spatial reasoning models, reveal a substantial gap in spatial reasoning capabilities compared with humans. In the multiple-choice setup, InternVL3.5-72B achieves 54.93% accuracy versus 87.57% for humans. In the open-ended setting, all models show a performance drop of around 10–25%, with GPT-5-mini scoring highest at 40.93% versus 64.93% for humans. These results highlight key limitations in handling complex spatial relationships, depth perception, navigation, and 3D geometry.
By providing a diverse, real-world evaluation framework, **_SpatiaLab_** exposes critical challenges and opportunities for advancing VLMs’ spatial reasoning, offering a benchmark to guide future research toward robust, human-aligned spatial understanding. We will release **_SpatiaLab_**.

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建 **SpatiaLab** 基准数据集，包含1,400个真实场景的视觉问答对。
  - 覆盖6大类空间推理任务：相对位置、深度与遮挡、方向、大小与尺度、空间导航、3D几何。
  - 每类含5个子类，共30种任务类型，支持多选与开放问答。
  - 使用真实世界图像，确保场景复杂性和多样性。

title: Code2Bench: Scaling Source and Rigor for Dynamic Benchmark Construction
abstract:
The evaluation of code-generating Large Language Models (LLMs) is fundamentally constrained by two intertwined challenges: a reliance on static, easily contaminated problem sources and the use of superficial, low-rigor testing. This paper introduces a new benchmark construction philosophy, Dual Scaling, designed to systematically address both limitations. Our approach involves continuously
scaling the source of problems from dynamic, real-world code repositories and systematically scaling the rigor of tests via automated, high-coverage Property-Based Testing (PBT). We instantiate this philosophy in CODE2BENCH, an end-to-end framework that leverages Scope Graph analysis for principled dependency classification and a 100% branch coverage quality gate to ensure test suite integrity.
Using this framework, we construct CODE2BENCH-2509, a new benchmark suite with native instances in both Python and Java. Our extensive evaluation of 10 state-of-the-art LLMs on CODE2BENCH-2509, powered by a novel "diagnostic
fingerprint" visualization, yields three key insights: (1) models exhibit a fundamental performance gap, excelling at API application (Weakly Self-Contained tasks) but struggling with algorithmic synthesis (Self-Contained tasks); (2) a model’s
performance is profoundly shaped by the target language’s ecosystem, a nuance we are the first to systematically quantify; and (3) our rigorous, scaled testing is critical in uncovering an "illusion of correctness" prevalent in simpler benchmarks. Our work presents a robust, scalable, and diagnostic paradigm for the next generation of LLM evaluation in software engineering. The code, data, and results are available at https://code2bench.github.io/.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 当前代码生成大模型的评估受限于静态、易污染的问题来源和低严谨度的测试方法。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有基准测试容易让模型“骗过”简单测试，掩盖真实能力缺陷，导致评估结果不可靠。
  - 真实软件开发依赖动态、复杂的代码库和严谨的测试，评估也应匹配这种现实。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出“双尺度”构建哲学：动态扩展问题来源（从真实代码仓库提取）+ 自动化高覆盖率测试（基于属性的测试，PBT）。
  - 构建CODE2BENCH框架：使用Scope Graph分析代码依赖关系，实现精准任务分类。
  - 设置100%分支覆盖率作为测试质量门禁，确保测试严谨性。
  - 生成CODE2BENCH-2509基准，包含Python和Java的2509个真实任务实例。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 10个SOTA模型在新基准上表现差异显著，揭示真实能力差距。
  - 模型在API调用任务上表现好，但在算法生成任务上严重不足。
  - 语言生态（如库、框架）显著影响模型表现，首次系统量化此影响。
  - 严谨测试能发现“虚假正确”现象，提升评估可信度。
  - 适用于评估代码生成类大模型在真实开发场景下的能力。

title: MolecularIQ: Characterizing Chemical Reasoning Capabilities Through Symbolic Verification on Molecular Graphs
abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly applied to chemistry, tackling tasks such as molecular name conversion, captioning, text-guided generation, and property or reaction prediction. A molecule’s properties are fundamentally determined by its composition and structure, encoded in its molecular graph; thus, reasoning about molecular properties requires understanding and reasoning over the molecular structure. Yet, most existing benchmarks emphasize general chemical knowledge, rely on literature or surrogate labels that risk leakage or bias, or reduce evaluation to multiple-choice questions.
We introduce MolecularIQ, a molecular structure reasoning benchmark focused exclusively on symbolically verifiable tasks. 
MolecularIQ spans three orthogonal axes — molecular complexity, multi-task load, and reasoning complexity — covering feature counting, index-based feature attributions, and constrained generation.
MolecularIQ enables fine-grained evaluation of reasoning over molecular graphs and produces capability fingerprints that localize model failures to specific tasks and molecular regimes. This provides actionable insights into the strengths and limitations of current chemistry LLMs and guides the development of models that reason faithfully over molecular structure.
On MolecularIQ, large MoE models with higher reasoning budgets lead across categories, while chemistry-tuned LLMs underperform their generalist bases, indicating limited transfer from narrow task fine-tuning.

- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 评估化学大模型在分子结构推理方面的真实能力，避免传统评测中因知识泄露或简化问题带来的偏差。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有化学评测多依赖文献或选择题，易受知识泄露影响，无法真实反映模型对分子结构的深层理解。
  - 模型对分子结构的推理能力是预测性质和反应的关键，需更精准、可验证的评估方式。
  - 当前模型在结构推理上表现不一，需定位具体短板以指导改进。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建MolecularIQ：一个专注于分子图结构符号可验证的任务集。
  - 任务覆盖三个维度：分子复杂度、多任务负载、推理复杂度。
  - 包含特征计数、基于索引的属性归因、约束生成等任务，均支持形式化验证。
  - 通过符号验证确保答案正确性，避免主观判断。

title: Omni-iEEG: A Large-Scale, Comprehensive iEEG Dataset and Benchmark for Epilepsy Research
abstract:
Epilepsy affects over 50 million people worldwide, and one-third of patients suffer drug-resistant seizures where surgery offers the best chance of seizure freedom. Accurate localization of the epileptogenic zone (EZ) relies on intracranial EEG (iEEG). Clinical workflows, however, remain constrained by labor-intensive manual review. At the same time, existing data-driven approaches are typically developed on single-center datasets that are inconsistent in format and metadata, lack standardized benchmarks, and rarely release pathological event annotations, creating barriers to reproducibility, cross-center validation, and clinical relevance. With extensive efforts to reconcile heterogeneous iEEG formats, metadata, and recordings across publicly available sources,
we present $\textbf{Omni-iEEG}$, a large-scale, pre-surgical iEEG resource comprising $\textbf{302 patients}$ and $\textbf{178 hours}$ of high-resolution recordings. The dataset includes harmonized clinical metadata such as seizure onset zones, resections, and surgical outcomes, all validated by board-certified epileptologists. In addition, Omni-iEEG provides over 36K expert-validated annotations of pathological events, enabling robust biomarker studies. Omni-iEEG serves as a bridge between machine learning and epilepsy research. It defines clinically meaningful tasks with unified evaluation metrics grounded in clinical priors, enabling systematic evaluation of models in clinically relevant settings. Beyond benchmarking, we demonstrate the potential of end-to-end modeling on long iEEG segments and highlight the transferability of representations pretrained on non-neurophysiological domains. Together, these contributions establish Omni-iEEG as a foundation for reproducible, generalizable, and clinically translatable epilepsy research.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 构建一个大规模、标准化的颅内脑电（iEEG）数据集，以支持癫痫研究中的机器学习应用。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 临床癫痫研究依赖iEEG定位致痫区，但传统人工分析耗时费力。
  - 现有数据集多来自单一中心，格式不统一、缺乏标准标注和临床验证，阻碍模型可复现性和跨中心应用。
  - 缺乏统一的基准测试框架，限制了机器学习模型在真实临床场景中的评估与落地。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 整合多个公开来源的iEEG数据，统一格式、元数据和标注标准。
  - 收集302名患者的高质量iEEG数据，总时长约178小时，涵盖多种癫痫类型。
  - 由专业癫痫医生验证临床信息（如发作起始区、手术切除范围、术后结果）。
  - 提供超过3.6万条专家标注的病理事件（如癫痫放电），支持精准建模。
  - 定义基于临床意义的任务与统一评估指标，支持端到端模型训练与迁移学习。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - Omni-iEEG是目前最大、最全面的预手术iEEG数据集之一，具备临床相关性与可复现性。
  - 支持端到端模型在长段iEEG上的有效建模，提升致痫区识别能力。
  - 预训练模型在非神经生理数据上学习的表征具有跨域迁移潜力。
  - 适用于癫痫研究、算法开发、临床验证与多中心协作。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首个大规模、多中心、标准化的iEEG数据集，实现数据格式与标注的统一。
  - 首次提供大量专家验证的病理事件标注，增强模型训练质量。
  - 建立基于临床先验的统一任务定义与评估标准，推动机器学习与临床实践对接。
  - 开放共享，促进可复现、可迁移、可验证的癫痫AI研究生态。

title: PILOT-Bench: Probabilistic Interaction for LLM Operations in Tool-driven Scenarios
abstract: We introduce PILOT-Bench, a benchmark that evaluates LLM workflow execution under simulated realistic conditions of instruction quality variability and tool execution uncertainty. Unlike existing benchmarks that encounter these challenges incidentally, our work makes uncertainty the primary focus of systematic study. The benchmark incorporates three key aspects: (1) modeling of probabilistic tool behaviors through parameterized error models that simulate real-world API failure patterns, (2) provision of MDP-derived workflows that maximize expected success rates, and (3) systematic evaluation of model robustness through controlled perturbations of workflow instruction quality. Our construction pipeline generates 5,040 tasks from a tool library of 30 APIs. The evaluation conducted across widely used large language models under conditions of probabilistic tool failures and varying instruction quality reveals notable performance differences. Specifically, MDP-optimal workflow prompts achieve an average success rate of 62.1\%, Chain-of-Thought prompts yield an average success rate of 50.8\%, and flawed workflow prompts result in an average success rate of 54.3\%. Our benchmark is available at \url{https://github.com/PilotBenchAnonymous/PilotBench}.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 评估大语言模型在工具调用场景下，面对工具执行不确定性和指令质量波动时的可靠表现。

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 现实中工具API常出现失败、响应异常，且用户指令质量参差不齐，但现有评测基准未系统性地模拟这些挑战。  
  - 需要一个能真实反映LLM在复杂任务中“抗干扰”能力的评测标准。

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 构建包含30个API的工具库，通过参数化误差模型模拟真实API失败模式（如随机失败、延迟等）。  
  - 用马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）生成最优任务流程，提升成功概率。  
  - 对指令质量进行可控扰动，测试模型对模糊或错误指令的鲁棒性。  
  - 生成5040个任务，系统评估多个主流LLM在不同条件下的表现。

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - MDP优化的提示词使平均成功率提升至62.1%，显著优于传统Chain-of-Thought（50.8%）和有缺陷提示词（54.3%）。  
  - 模型对指令质量和工具不确定性极为敏感，说明当前LLM在真实场景中仍不稳定。  
  - 适用于评估和改进LLM在自动化工具调用任务中的可靠性。

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首个将“工具执行不确定性”和“指令质量波动”作为核心评测维度的基准。  
  - 引入参数化误差模型与MDP生成最优流程，使评测更具真实性和可复现性。  
  - 提供可扩展的构建管道，支持未来更多工具与场景的评测。

title: The Tool Decathlon: Benchmarking Language Agents for Diverse, Realistic, and Long-Horizon Task Execution
abstract:
Real-world language agents must handle complex, multi-step workflows across diverse applications. For instance, an agent may manage emails by coordinating with calendars and file systems, or monitor a production database like BigQuery to detect anomalies and generate reports following a standard operating manual.  However, existing language agent benchmarks often focus on narrow domains or simplified tasks that lack the diversity, realism, and long-horizon complexity required to evaluate agents' real-world performance. 
To address this gap, we introduce the Tool Decathlon (dubbed as Toolathlon), a benchmark for language agents offering diverse applications and tools, realistic environment setup, and reliable execution-based evaluation. Toolathlon spans 32 software applications and 604 tools, ranging from everyday platforms such as Google Calendar and Notion to professional applications like WooCommerce, Kubernetes, and BigQuery.  Most of the tools are based on a high-quality set of Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers that we may have revised or implemented ourselves. Unlike prior works, which primarily ensure functional realism but offer limited environment state diversity, we provide realistic initial environment states from real software, such as multiple Canvas courses each with dozens of students or real-world financial spreadsheets. The Toolathlon benchmark includes 108 manually sourced or crafted tasks in total, requiring interacting with multiple applications over ~20 interaction turns on average to complete.  Each task is strictly verifiable through dedicated evaluation scripts. Comprehensive evaluation of state-of-the-art models highlights their significant shortcomings in performing real-world, long-horizon tasks: the best-performing model, Claude-4-Sonnet, achieves only a 29.9% success rate with 28 tool calling turns on average, while the top open-weights model DeepSeek-V3.1 reaches 13.9%. We expect Toolathlon to drive the development of more capable language agents for real-world, long-horizon task execution.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 评估语言代理在真实、复杂、多步骤任务中的执行能力，尤其是跨多种应用和工具的长期任务。

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 现有基准测试过于狭窄或简化，无法真实反映语言代理在现实世界中处理多应用、长流程任务的能力。  
  - 缺乏多样化的初始环境状态和可靠的执行验证机制，导致评估结果不可靠。

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 构建 Tool Decathlon（Toolathlon）基准，涵盖 32 个真实软件应用（如 Google Calendar、BigQuery、Kubernetes）和 604 个工具。  
  - 所有工具基于高质量的 Model Context Protocol (MCP) 服务器实现或优化，确保功能真实。  
  - 提供来自真实场景的初始环境状态（如多个课程、真实财务表格），增强环境多样性。  
  - 设计 108 个手工创建的任务，平均需 20 步交互完成，每项任务有专用验证脚本确保可验证性。

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - 当前最先进模型表现有限：Claude-4-Sonnet 成功率为 29.9%，DeepSeek-V3.1 仅 13.9%。  
  - 表明现有语言代理在处理真实、复杂、长周期任务时仍存在显著能力差距。  
  - 适用于评估和推动下一代语言代理在真实工作流中的发展。

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首个融合多样性、真实性与长周期任务的综合性语言代理基准。  
  - 引入真实软件的初始环境状态，提升评估场景的现实感。

title: VeriEquivBench: An Equivalence Score for Ground-Truth-Free Evaluation of Formally Verifiable Code
abstract:
Formal verification is the next frontier for ensuring the correctness of code generated by Large Language Models (LLMs). 
While methods that co-generate code and formal specifications in formal languages, like Dafny, can, in principle, prove alignment with user intent, progress is bottlenecked by specification quality evaluation. 
Current benchmarks rely on matching against ground-truth specifications, a manual and expertise-intensive process that has limited existing datasets to a few hundred simple problems and also suffers from a reliability issue.
To address this, we introduce VeriEquivBench, a new benchmark with $2,389$ complex algorithmic problems that probe the limitations of current models in both code generation and formal reasoning. 
Our evaluation framework replaces ground-truth matching with a formally grounded metric, the equivalence score, and rigorously verifies the quality of generated specifications and code.
Our results show that generating formally verifiable code remains a profound challenge for state-of-the-art LLMs. This underscores both the difficulty of the task and the need for benchmarks like VeriEquivBench to drive progress toward scalable and reliable coding agents.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在没有真实参考规范的情况下，评估大语言模型生成的代码及其形式化规范的正确性。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有评估依赖人工标注的“真实规范”，成本高、可扩展性差，且易出错。
  - 现有数据集规模小（仅数百个简单问题），无法真实反映模型在复杂算法上的表现。
  - 需要一种无需依赖人工参考、自动且可靠的评估方式，以推动形式化代码生成的发展。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出“等价得分”（equivalence score）：通过形式化验证工具自动判断生成的代码与原始需求是否逻辑等价。
  - 构建包含 2,389 个复杂算法问题的基准数据集，覆盖多种算法场景。
  - 使用形式化验证器（如 Dafny）对生成的代码和规范进行自动验证，替代人工比对。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 当前最先进的 LLMs 在生成可验证代码方面仍表现不佳，说明该任务极具挑战性。
  - VeriEquivBench 能有效评估模型在复杂场景下的形式化推理能力。
  - 适用于评估未来可验证代码生成系统，尤其适合研究 LLMs 与形式化方法结合的场景。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次提出无需“真实规范”的自动评估框架，摆脱对人工标注的依赖。
  - 引入形式化验证驱动的等价得分，实现更可靠、可扩展的评估。
  - 构建大规模、复杂度高的新基准数据集，填补现有空白。

title: Benchmarking Stochastic Approximation Algorithms for Fairness-Constrained Training of Deep Neural Networks
abstract: The ability to train Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) with constraints is instrumental in improving the fairness of modern machine-learning models. Many algorithms have been analysed in recent years, and yet there is no standard, widely accepted method for the constrained training of DNNs. In this paper, we provide a challenging benchmark of real-world large-scale fairness-constrained learning tasks, built on top of the US Census (Folktables, Ding et al, 2021). We point out the theoretical challenges of such tasks and review the main approaches in stochastic approximation algorithms. Finally, we demonstrate the use of the benchmark by implementing and comparing three recently proposed, but as-of-yet unimplemented, algorithms both in terms of optimization performance, and fairness improvement. We will release the code of the benchmark as a Python package after peer-review.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何在深度神经网络训练中有效实现公平性约束，且缺乏统一可靠的评估方法。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 公平性约束对防止模型歧视至关重要，但现有算法缺乏统一评估标准，难以比较优劣。  
  - 现实世界的大规模公平性任务存在复杂性，亟需一个标准化的基准测试平台。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 构建基于美国人口普查数据（Folktables）的大型真实世界公平性约束学习任务作为基准。  
  - 选取三种近期提出但未被实现的随机近似算法进行对比实验。  
  - 通过优化性能与公平性提升双重指标，系统评估各算法表现。

- 结论是什么？  
  - 所提出的基准能有效区分不同算法在真实场景下的优劣。

title: NC-Bench and NCfold: A Benchmark and Closed-Loop Framework for RNA Non-Canonical Base-Pair Prediction
abstract:
RNA secondary structure forms the basis for folding and function, with non-canonical (NC) interactions indispensable for catalysis, regulation, and molecular recognition.
Despite their importance, predicting NC base pairs remains challenging due to the absence of a standardized benchmark for systematic evaluation.
To address this, we introduce NC-Bench, the first benchmark dedicated to NC base-pair prediction. NC-Bench provides 925 curated RNA sequences with 6,708 high-quality NC annotations, fine-grained edge and orientation classification tasks, and IsoScore-based embedding evaluation, offering a rigorous foundation for systematic assessment.
Building on this, we propose NCfold, a dual-branch framework that couples sequence features with structural priors derived from RNA foundation models (RFMs) via Representative Embedding Fusion (REF) and REF-weighted self-attention.
The closed-loop design iteratively refines sequence and structure representations, alleviating data sparsity and enhancing predictive accuracy. 
Experiments on NC-Bench show that NCfold outperforms existing methods, with zero-shot and ablation studies confirming its effectiveness and underscoring the need for NC-specific benchmarks.
Together, NC-Bench and NCfold establish a systematic foundation for NC base-pair prediction, advancing our understanding of RNA structure and enabling next-generation RNA-centric applications. The datasets and codes are publicly available at https://github.com/heqin-zhu/NCBench.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何准确预测RNA分子中非经典碱基配对（NC base pairs）这一关键结构特征。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 非经典碱基配对在RNA催化、调控和识别中至关重要，但缺乏统一标准的评估基准，导致方法难以比较和进步。  
  - 现有预测方法受限于数据稀疏性和结构先验不足，准确率不高。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 构建NC-Bench：首个专门针对NC碱基配对的基准数据集，包含925条高质量RNA序列和6,708个标注，支持细粒度分类与嵌入评估。  
  - 提出NCfold：双分支框架，融合序列信息与来自RNA基础模型的结构先验，通过“代表性嵌入融合”（REF）和加权自注意力机制协同优化。  
  - 采用闭环设计，迭代更新序列与结构表示，缓解数据稀疏问题。

- 结论是什么？  
  - NCfold在NC-Bench上显著优于现有方法，零样本和消融实验验证其有效性。  
  - 证明了专用基准对推动该领域发展的必要性。  
  - 适用于RNA结构预测、功能解析及新药设计等下游任务。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次提出面向NC碱基配对的标准化基准NC-Bench，填补领域空白。  
  - 设计闭环式NCfold框架，巧妙融合序列与结构先验，提升预测性能。

title: OpenEstimate: Evaluating LLMs on Probabilistic Estimation with Real-World Data
abstract: Real-world settings where language models (LMs) are deployed---in domains spanning healthcare, finance, and other forms of knowledge work—require models to grapple with incomplete information and reason under uncertainty. Yet most LM evaluations focus on problems with well-defined answers and success criteria. This gap exists in part because natural problems involving uncertainty are difficult to construct: given that LMs have access to most of the same knowledge as humans, it is non-trivial to design questions for which LMs will struggle to produce correct answers, but which humans can answer reliably.As a result, LM performance on reasoning under uncertainty remains poorly characterized. To address this gap, we introduce \textsc{OpenEstimate}, an extensible, multi-domain benchmark for evaluating LMs on numerical estimation tasks that require models to synthesize significant amounts of background information and express predictions as probabilistic priors. We assess these priors for accuracy and calibration. Across six frontier models, we find that LM-elicited priors are worth the equivalent of about five samples from the underlying data distribution, and that posteriors computed using LM priors tend to be more accurate than those computed using a naive prior. At the same time, the relationship between model accuracy and confidence is weak across the board, indicating the value of developing new methods to improve calibration. The \textsc{OpenEstimate} benchmark thus offers a challenging evaluation for frontier LMs and a platform for developing models that are better at probabilistic estimation and reasoning under uncertainty.
explain_zh:
- 评估大模型在真实世界中进行概率估计的能力，尤其是在信息不全、需要推理不确定性的场景下。
- 现有评估大多针对答案明确的任务，难以衡量模型在不确定环境下的表现。
- 提出 OpenEstimate 基准，涵盖多个领域，要求模型基于背景知识给出概率性预测（如“某事件发生的概率在 30%-70%”）。
- 通过真实世界数据验证模型预测的准确性与校准性（即自信程度是否与真实准确率匹配）。
- 实验发现：大模型给出的概率先验相当于约 5 个真实样本的价值，且用其作为先验能提升后验估计的准确性。
- 模型自信程度与实际准确率关联弱，说明当前模型校准能力差，需改进。

1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 评估大语言模型在真实世界中处理不确定信息、进行概率性估计的能力。

2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有模型评估多聚焦于“标准答案”任务，无法反映模型在信息不全、需推理不确定性的实际场景中的表现。  
- 真实问题中，人类能基于背景知识合理估计，但设计这类问题对模型有挑战性，现有评估体系缺失。

3) 方法是什么？  
- 构建 OpenEstimate 基准，涵盖多个现实领域（如医疗、金融），提出需模型综合背景知识并输出概率区间（如“概率在 30%-70%”）的估计任务。  
- 评估模型输出的“先验”是否准确（与真实数据一致）和是否校准（自信程度与实际正确率匹配）。  
- 用真实数据计算后验，验证模型先验对最终估计的提升作用。

4) 结论是什么？  
- 大模型给出的概率先验相当于约 5 个真实样本的信息价值。  
- 用模型先验能提升后验估计的准确性。  
- 模型自信程度与真实准确率关联弱，说明其校准能力差，需改进。

5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首个面向真实世界、多领域、可扩展的概率估计基准，聚焦不确定性推理。  
- 引入“概率先验”作为评估核心，推动模型从“给出答案”转向“合理估计不确定性”。

title: PACEbench: A Framework for Evaluating Practical AI Cyber-Exploitation Capabilities
abstract:
The increasing autonomy of Large Language Models (LLMs) necessitates a rigorous evaluation of their potential to aid in cyber offense. Existing benchmarks often lack real-world complexity and are thus unable to accurately assess LLMs' cybersecurity capabilities. To address this gap, we introduce PACEbench, a practical AI cyber-exploitation benchmark built on the principles of realistic vulnerability difficulty, environmental complexity, and cyber defenses. Specifically, PACEbench comprises four scenarios spanning single, blended, chained, and defense vulnerability exploitations. To handle these complex challenges, we propose PACEagent, a novel agent that emulates human penetration testers by supporting multi-phase reconnaissance, analysis, and exploitation.
Extensive experiments with seven frontier LLMs demonstrate that current models struggle with complex cyber scenarios, and none can bypass defenses. These findings suggest that current models do not yet pose a generalized cyber offense threat. Nonetheless, our work provides a robust benchmark to guide the trustworthy development of future models.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 评估大型语言模型（LLMs）在真实复杂网络环境中进行网络攻击的实用能力。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有评估基准过于简单，无法反映真实世界中漏洞利用的复杂性与防御机制。
  - LLM自主性增强，可能被滥用用于网络攻击，需提前评估其潜在风险。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建 PACEbench：包含四种真实场景——单一漏洞、混合漏洞、链式漏洞、带防御的漏洞利用。
  - 提出 PACEagent：模拟人类渗透测试员的智能体，支持多阶段操作（侦察、分析、攻击）。
  - 使用真实漏洞和防御机制构建环境，评估 LLM 在复杂任务中的表现。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 七款前沿 LLM 在复杂场景中表现不佳，均无法绕过防御机制。
  - 当前 LLM 尚不具备通用化网络攻击能力，未构成广泛威胁。
  - 为未来可信 AI 模型开发提供可靠评估工具，适用于安全研究与模型审计。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）

title: SOSBENCH: Benchmarking Safety Alignment on Scientific Knowledge
abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) exhibit advancing capabilities in complex tasks, such as reasoning and graduate-level question answering, yet their resilience against misuse, particularly involving scientifically sophisticated risks, remains underexplored. Existing safety benchmarks typically focus either on instructions requiring minimal knowledge comprehension (e.g., ``tell me how to build a bomb") or utilize prompts that are relatively low-risk (e.g., multiple-choice or classification tasks about hazardous content). Consequently, they fail to adequately assess model safety when handling knowledge-intensive, hazardous scenarios.

To address this critical gap, we introduce SOSBench, a regulation-grounded, hazard-focused benchmark encompassing six high-risk scientific domains: chemistry, biology, medicine, pharmacology, physics, and psychology. The benchmark comprises 3,000 prompts derived from real-world regulations and laws, systematically expanded via an LLM-assisted evolutionary pipeline that introduces diverse, realistic misuse scenarios (e.g., detailed explosive synthesis instructions involving advanced chemical formulas). We evaluate frontier models within a unified evaluation framework using our SOSBench. Despite their alignment claims, advanced models consistently disclose policy-violating content across all domains, demonstrating alarmingly high rates of harmful responses (e.g., 84.9% for Deepseek-R1 and 50.3% for GPT-4.1). These results highlight significant safety alignment deficiencies and underscore urgent concerns regarding the responsible deployment of powerful LLMs.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 评估大语言模型在涉及复杂科学知识的高风险场景下的安全对齐能力。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有安全评测多针对简单指令或低风险任务，无法真实反映模型在科学复杂场景下的滥用风险。
  - 高风险科学领域（如化学合成、药物研发）的不当使用可能造成严重现实危害，亟需更精准的安全评估。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建SOSBench：基于真实法规和法律条文生成3000个高风险科学场景提示。
  - 采用LLM辅助的演化流程，自动扩展出多样化、贴近现实的滥用情境（如详细爆炸物合成步骤）。
  - 在统一框架下评估前沿模型，量化其输出有害内容的比例。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）

title: Internal Evaluation of Density-Based Clusterings with Noise
abstract:
Evaluating the quality of a clustering result without access to ground truth labels is fundamental for research in data mining.
However, most cluster validation indices (CVIs) do not consider the noise assignments by density-based clustering methods like DBSCAN or HDBSCAN, even though the ability to correctly determine noise is paramount to successful clustering. 
In this paper, we propose DISCO, a **D**ensity-based **I**nternal **S**core for **C**lusterings with n**O**ise, the first CVI to explicitly assess the *quality* of noise assignments rather than merely counting them.
DISCO is based on the Silhouette Coefficient, but adopts density-connectivity to evaluate clusters of arbitrary shapes, and proposes explicit noise evaluation: it rewards correctly assigned noise labels and penalizes noise labels where a cluster label would have been more appropriate.
The pointwise definition of DISCO allows for the seamless integration of noise evaluation into the final clustering evaluation, while also enabling explainable evaluations of the clustered data.
In contrast to most state-of-the-art, DISCO is well-defined and also covers edge cases that regularly appear as output from clustering algorithms, such as singleton clusters or a single cluster plus noise.
explain_zh:
We evaluate DISCO on a wide range of synthetic and real-world datasets, and compare it to 11 state-of-the-art CVIs. Results show that DISCO consistently outperforms existing indices in identifying the optimal clustering configuration, especially in the presence of noise. DISCO is also shown to be robust to parameter choices and to be sensitive to the quality of noise assignments.

- **解决的问题是什么？**  
  - 如何在没有真实标签的情况下，准确评估密度聚类方法（如DBSCAN）对“噪声点”的识别质量。

- **动机是什么？**  
  - 传统聚类评估指标只关注聚类内部结构，忽略噪声点的正确性；而密度聚类的核心能力之一就是识别噪声，现有方法无法衡量噪声判断的好坏。  
  - 现实数据中噪声普遍存在，错误地将噪声点归为簇或反之都会严重影响聚类效果。

- **方法是什么？**  
  - 提出DISCO：首个专门评估噪声质量的内部聚类评估指标。  
  - 基于轮廓系数（Silhouette）思想，但用“密度连通性”替代距离，适用于任意形状的簇。  
  - 显式评估每个点是否被正确标记为噪声：对正确识别的噪声点奖励，对本应属于某簇却被标为噪声的点惩罚。  
  - 支持点级评估，可解释性强，能处理单点簇、全噪声等边缘情况。

- **结论是什么？**  
  - 在多种合成与真实数据集上，DISCO显著优于11个主流评估指标，尤其在噪声存在时表现更优。  
  - 对聚类参数选择不敏感，能有效反映噪声判断质量，适合实际应用。

- **创新点是什么？**  
  - 首次将“噪声质量”作为核心评估目标，突破传统指标只看簇内结构的局限。  
  - 通过密度连通性与点级惩罚机制，实现对噪声的精准量化评估。  
  - 设计鲁棒、可解释，且能处理复杂边缘情况，实用性更强。

title: WARC-Bench: Web Archive based Benchmark for GUI Subtask Executions
abstract: Training web agents to navigate complex, real-world websites requires them to master subtasks—short-horizon interactions on multiple UI components (e.g., choosing the correct date in a date picker, or scrolling in a container to extract information). We introduce WARC-Bench (Web Archive Benchmark), a novel web navigation benchmark featuring 438 tasks designed to evaluate multimodal AI agents on subtasks. WARC-Bench enables sandboxed interactions with dynamic and realistic webpages using Web ARChive files. We show that WARC-Bench is challenging for leading computer-use models, with the highest observed success rate being 64.8%. To improve open source models on subtask, we explore two common training techniques: supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR). Experiments show that SFT models obtain a 48.8% success rate on the benchmark. Training with RLVR over SFT checkpoints, even in data-scarce settings, improves the score to 52.8% on WARC-Bench, outperforming many frontier models. Our analysis concludes that mastering these subtasks is essential for robust web planning and navigation, and is a capability not extensively evaluated by existing benchmarks.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 构建一个能真实评估AI在复杂网页上执行细粒度操作（如选择日期、滚动页面）能力的基准测试平台。  

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 现有网页导航评测缺乏对“子任务”（subtasks）的细致考察，而这些小操作是完成复杂任务的关键；  
  - 真实网页动态性强，传统测试环境难以复现，导致模型评估不真实；  
  - 当前主流模型在细粒度交互上表现不佳，亟需更精准的评估与训练工具。  

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 基于Web ARChive（WARC）文件构建可复现的网页环境，支持沙箱化、动态交互；  
  - 设计438个真实世界子任务，覆盖多UI组件操作（如日期选择器、滚动容器）；  
  - 采用监督微调（SFT）和可验证奖励强化学习（RLVR）训练AI模型，提升子任务执行能力。  

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - WARC-Bench极具挑战性，顶尖模型最高成功率为64.8%；  
  - SFT模型在该基准上达48.8%成功率，RLVR进一步提升至52.8%，优于许多先进模型；  
  - 子任务能力对整体网页导航至关重要，现有基准未充分评估此能力。  

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首个基于真实网页存档（WARC）构建的子任务评估基准，实现真实、可复现的测试环境；  
  - 明确提出并验证“子任务能力”是网页导航的核心瓶颈，推动评估范式升级；  
  - 在数据稀缺下验证RLVR的有效性，为小样本场景下的网页智能训练提供新路径。

title: Tab-MIA: A Benchmark Dataset for Membership Inference Attacks on Tabular Data in LLMs
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly trained on tabular data, which, unlike unstructured text, often contains personally identifiable information (PII) in a highly structured and explicit format. As a result, privacy risks arise, since sensitive records can be inadvertently retained by the model and exposed through data extraction or membership inference attacks (MIAs). While existing MIA methods primarily target textual content, their efficacy and threat implications may differ when applied to structured data, due to its limited content, diverse data types, unique value distributions, and column-level semantics. In this paper, we present Tab-MIA, a benchmark dataset for evaluating MIAs on tabular data in LLMs and demonstrate how it can be used. Tab-MIA comprises five data collections, each represented in six different encoding formats. Using our Tab-MIA benchmark, we conduct the first evaluation of state-of-the-art MIA methods on LLMs fine-tuned with tabular data across multiple encoding formats. In the evaluation, we analyze the memorization behavior of pretrained LLMs on structured data derived from Wikipedia tables. Our findings show that LLMs memorize tabular data in ways that vary across encoding formats, making them susceptible to extraction via MIAs. Even when fine-tuned for as few as three epochs, models exhibit high vulnerability, with AUROC scores approaching 90% in most cases. Tab-MIA enables systematic evaluation of these risks and provides a foundation for developing privacy-preserving methods for tabular data in LLMs.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 针对大语言模型（LLMs）在处理表格数据时可能泄露敏感信息的问题，提出一种评估成员推断攻击（MIA）风险的基准数据集。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 表格数据常含个人身份信息（PII），且结构化程度高，易被模型“记住”；
  - 现有MIA方法主要针对文本，不适用于表格数据，缺乏专门评估工具；
  - 需要系统性地衡量LLMs在表格数据上的隐私风险，以推动安全防护研究。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建Tab-MIA基准数据集，包含5个真实数据集，每种数据有6种编码格式（如JSON、CSV、表格文本等）；
  - 使用预训练LLM在这些数据上微调，模拟真实应用场景；
  - 采用多种主流MIA方法对微调后的模型进行攻击测试，评估其隐私泄露程度。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - LLMs即使仅微调3个epoch，对表格数据仍高度易受MIA攻击，AUROC达90%；
  - 不同编码格式影响模型记忆行为，进而影响攻击成功率；
  - 该数据集可广泛用于评估和改进LLMs在表格数据中的隐私保护能力。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首个专为LLMs处理表格数据设计的MIA基准数据集；
  - 提供多格式编码的标准化测试平台，揭示编码方式对隐私风险的影响；
  - 推动了表格数据隐私研究从“文本导向”向“结构化数据导向”的转变。
